question: Will Chris Wright be the next to leave the Trump Cabinet before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve based on the next individual announced to leave the Trump Cabinet, or who otherwise ceases to be a member of the administration. If no one leaves by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”.

An announcement of an individual's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the resignation/removal takes effect.

If multiple individuals announce departures or are removed at the same time, the market will resolve to the individual who actually leaves office first. If they leave simultaneously, the market will resolve to the individual whose last name comes first alphabetically.

For the purposes of this market, the Cabinet includes Vice President, the heads of the 15 executive departments, as well as the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the President’s Chief of Staff, the Director of National Intelligence (DNI), the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), the United States Trade Representative (USTR), the Ambassador to the United Nations, the Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA), and the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA). Acting officials serving in these roles are not included. The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) is not considered Cabinet-level under the current Trump administration and is excluded from this market.

An individual will be considered to have left the Cabinet if they resign from or are removed from any Cabinet-level position, even if they are subsequently appointed to a different Cabinet-level role.

If a candidate who is not already listed, assumes a listed cabinet position they will be added to the market. However, candidates who have merely been nominated for a cabinet position will not be considered to have left, even if their nomination is rejected or withdrawn.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reopening may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Lori Chavez-DeRemer at 48% to be the next Trump Cabinet departure, primarily due to intensifying GOP opposition to her Labor Secretary nomination over her moderate House voting record, including support for infrastructure funding, Ukraine aid, and 2020 election certification. Recent statements from senators like Tommy Tuberville calling for her withdrawal, despite President Trump's public defense, have heightened confirmation risks ahead of Senate hearings. Tulsi Gabbard ranks second at 11.3%, reflecting lingering intelligence community skepticism toward her Director of National Intelligence bid, while Pam Bondi at 9% benefits from stronger Republican backing for Attorney General. Odds for no departures before 2027 remain low at 6.8%, underscoring trader expectations of early turbulence.

An announcement of an individual's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the resignation/removal takes effect.

If multiple individuals announce departures or are removed at the same time, the market will resolve to the individual who actually leaves office first. If they leave simultaneously, the market will resolve to the individual whose last name comes first alphabetically.

For the purposes of this market, the Cabinet includes Vice President, the heads of the 15 executive departments, as well as the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the President’s Chief of Staff, the Director of National Intelligence (DNI), the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), the United States Trade Representative (USTR), the Ambassador to the United Nations, the Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA), and the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA). Acting officials serving in these roles are not included. The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) is not considered Cabinet-level under the current Trump administration and is excluded from this market.

An individual will be considered to have left the Cabinet if they resign from or are removed from any Cabinet-level position, even if they are subsequently appointed to a different Cabinet-level role.

If a candidate who is not already listed, assumes a listed cabinet position they will be added to the market. However, candidates who have merely been nominated for a cabinet position will not be considered to have left, even if their nomination is rejected or withdrawn.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reopening may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Lori Chavez-DeRemer at 48% to be the next Trump Cabinet departure, primarily due to intensifying GOP opposition to her Labor Secretary nomination over her moderate House voting record, including support for infrastructure funding, Ukraine aid, and 2020 election certification. Recent statements from senators like Tommy Tuberville calling for her withdrawal, despite President Trump's public defense, have heightened confirmation risks ahead of Senate hearings. Tulsi Gabbard ranks second at 11.3%, reflecting lingering intelligence community skepticism toward her Director of National Intelligence bid, while Pam Bondi at 9% benefits from stronger Republican backing for Attorney General. Odds for no departures before 2027 remain low at 6.8%, underscoring trader expectations of early turbulence.

question: Will Freiburg reach the UEFA Europa League final?
description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed team reaches the 2026 UEFA Europa League final. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to advance to the UEFA Europa League final (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Europa League is cancelled, postponed after June 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Europa League final matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dortmund's 1-0 first-leg upset over PSG has traders pricing them as narrow favorites to advance from the semi-final tie, while Real Madrid's gritty 2-2 draw at Bayern Munich reinforces their status as overwhelming consensus picks to reach the Wembley final on May 31. PSG faces do-or-die pressure at home on May 7 with Mbappé pivotal but their defense exposed, and Bayern's second leg at the Bernabéu on May 8 looms amid injury concerns for Neuer (doubtful after training) and Davies (out). Real's flawless knockout pedigree and home-edge momentum, against Dortmund's resilient counter-attacking form, drive current market probabilities reflecting crowd wisdom on aggregate outcomes.

question: Will Gentner Drummond replace Markwayne Mullin as United States Senator from Oklahoma?
description:
On March 5, 2026, Donald Trump announced that the United States Senator from Oklahoma, Markwayne Mullin, will be appointed to replace Kristi Noem as United States Secretary of Homeland Security.

This market will resolve according to the next person officially appointed by the Governor of Oklahoma, or otherwise formally selected according the jurisdiction's vacancy rules, to fill Markwayne Mullin’s seat as United States Senator from Oklahoma.

Interim, Acting, or other temporary Senators appointed or elected to the role will count.

If no replacement senator has been appointed or elected by November 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None Before Midterm Election.”

Additionally, if, prior to November 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a widespread consensus of credible reporting indicates that Markwayne Mullin will complete his term as United States Senator (e.g. if Mullin’s nomination as Secretary of Homeland Security is rejected or withdrawn and he announces he will complete his term), this market will resolve immediately to “None Before Midterm Election.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Oklahoma; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Who will replace Mullin as Oklahoma Senator?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Oklahoma House Speaker Charles McCall at 34% to replace Sen. Markwayne Mullin, following President-elect Trump's nomination of Mullin for Interior Secretary, which would trigger Gov. Kevin Stitt's appointment of an interim replacement until the 2026 special election. McCall's strong legislative record and ties to Stitt differentiate him from State Supt. Ryan Walters (23%), whose Trump alignment and education policy profile appeal to the base, while Lt. Gov. Matt Pinnell (10%) and AG Gentner Drummond (10%) lag with business and legal credentials respectively. Support could consolidate via Stitt's signals, Trump endorsements, or private polling, amid uncertainty over Senate confirmation timelines.

Interim, Acting, or other temporary Senators appointed or elected to the role will count.

If no replacement senator has been appointed or elected by November 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None Before Midterm Election.”

Additionally, if, prior to November 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a widespread consensus of credible reporting indicates that Markwayne Mullin will complete his term as United States Senator (e.g. if Mullin’s nomination as Secretary of Homeland Security is rejected or withdrawn and he announces he will complete his term), this market will resolve immediately to “None Before Midterm Election.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Oklahoma; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Oklahoma House Speaker Charles McCall at 34% to replace Sen. Markwayne Mullin, following President-elect Trump's nomination of Mullin for Interior Secretary, which would trigger Gov. Kevin Stitt's appointment of an interim replacement until the 2026 special election. McCall's strong legislative record and ties to Stitt differentiate him from State Supt. Ryan Walters (23%), whose Trump alignment and education policy profile appeal to the base, while Lt. Gov. Matt Pinnell (10%) and AG Gentner Drummond (10%) lag with business and legal credentials respectively. Support could consolidate via Stitt's signals, Trump endorsements, or private polling, amid uncertainty over Senate confirmation timelines.

question: Will a team from England be the 2026 Champions League winner?
description:
This market will resolve according to the home nation of the 2026 UEFA Champions League winner. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any club from the listed country to win the UEFA Champions League (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Champions League is cancelled, postponed after June 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Champions League champion has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Champions League: Home country of champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the home nation of the 2026 UEFA Champions League winner. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any club from the listed country to win the UEFA Champions League (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Champions League is cancelled, postponed after June 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Champions League champion has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The razor-thin gap between Spain (33%) and England (32%) underscores the depth of elite contenders from La Liga and the Premier League reaching the Champions League quarterfinals, with Real Madrid, Barcelona, and Atletico Madrid clashing against Manchester City and Arsenal amid tight head-to-head histories and peak form. Germany's 23% share reflects Bayern Munich and Borussia Dortmund's knockout resilience, including Dortmund's upset over Atletico, while France's PSG stands alone after advancing past Barcelona. Recent quarterfinal results eliminated English hopes but preserved Spanish primacy via Real's penalty shootout win over City, fueling trader consensus on league parity and unpredictable upsets as semifinals loom with Real vs. Bayern and Dortmund vs. PSG.

question: Will Taylor Swift have a #1 hit in April?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any song by the listed artist is in the #1 spot on the Spotify Top 50 - Global chart for any day of the relevant month, ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not qualify to resolve the featured artist to "Yes".

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify, specifically the Spotify Top 50 - Global chart, which can be found here: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZEVXbMDoHDwVN2tF.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taylor Swift's "Fortnight" has surged to the top of midweek charts after The Tortured Poets Department's blockbuster April 19 release, driving trader consensus to over 80% implied probability for her claiming April's final #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 dated April 27. Earlier in the month, Future, Metro Boomin, and Kendrick Lamar dominated with "Like That" holding #1 for three straight weeks amid the viral Drake feud buzz, fueled by massive streaming gains. Beyoncé's "Texas Hold 'Em" kicked off strong but faded; now, radio airplay and sales bundles will decide if Swift locks in or challengers like Billie Eilish's "Birds of a Feather" upset. Watch Friday's official chart for resolution.

question: Will a team from England be the 2026 Europa League winner?
description:
This market will resolve according to the home nation of the 2026 UEFA Europa League winner. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any club from the listed country to win the UEFA Europa League (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Europa League is cancelled, postponed after June 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Europa League champion has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Europa League: Home country of champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the home nation of the 2026 UEFA Europa League winner. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any club from the listed country to win the UEFA Europa League (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Europa League is cancelled, postponed after June 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Europa League champion has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Quarterfinal first-leg results dominate trader sentiment in the UEFA Europa League champion's home country market, with Bayer Leverkusen's 2-0 win over West Ham bolstering Germany's 11.5% implied probability amid their unbeaten season under Xabi Alonso, while Italy surges via Atalanta's stunning 3-0 rout of Liverpool and Fiorentina's 1-1 draw against Viktoria Plzeň. Portugal's Benfica holds a slim 2-1 edge over Marseille, supporting its 22.5% share. England clings to top consensus at 46% on Liverpool's Anfield second-leg potential despite the deficit and West Ham's resilience, as traders weigh Premier League depth against these shocks; Spain's 27.5% nods to historical prowess like Sevilla's seven titles, absent a current entrant.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chelsea's perfect start in the UEFA Europa Conference League phase, with nine points from three convincing wins over Gent, Panathinaikos, and Noah, has propelled England to the top of trader consensus at 35.5% implied probability for producing the champion, reflecting the Blues' deep squad depth and Premier League pedigree as overwhelming favorites. Italy (16.5%) gains from Fiorentina's recent final experience despite uneven form, while Netherlands (15.5%) benefits from Go Ahead Eagles and Heerenveen's solid results. Ukraine and France (both 14.5%) draw support from dark-horse contenders like Dynamo Kyiv and Saint-Étienne amid the league phase's unpredictability, with no major injuries yet shifting odds; Germany's Heidenheim and Spain's sides lag due to inconsistent performances.

If at any point it becomes impossible for any club from the listed country to win the UEFA Europa Conference League (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 UEFA Europa Conference League is cancelled, postponed after June 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 UEFA Europa Conference League champion has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chelsea's perfect start in the UEFA Europa Conference League phase, with nine points from three convincing wins over Gent, Panathinaikos, and Noah, has propelled England to the top of trader consensus at 35.5% implied probability for producing the champion, reflecting the Blues' deep squad depth and Premier League pedigree as overwhelming favorites. Italy (16.5%) gains from Fiorentina's recent final experience despite uneven form, while Netherlands (15.5%) benefits from Go Ahead Eagles and Heerenveen's solid results. Ukraine and France (both 14.5%) draw support from dark-horse contenders like Dynamo Kyiv and Saint-Étienne amid the league phase's unpredictability, with no major injuries yet shifting odds; Germany's Heidenheim and Spain's sides lag due to inconsistent performances.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF Femenino
events.description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for Thursday, April 2, 2026 between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF Femenino.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení's unmatched Liga F dominance, with an unbeaten streak spanning dozens of matches, clashes against Real Madrid Femenino's sharpened attack and resilience, fueling the tight trader consensus where draw odds lead at 49% ahead of narrow Barcelona (48%) and Madrid (47%) probabilities. Recent developments underscore the balance: Barcelona boasts stars like Salma Paralluelo in peak form post-international duty, but faces questions over Caroline Graham Hansen's minor knock, while Madrid integrates new signings like Caroline Weir effectively, riding a four-match win streak including tough away results. Head-to-head history favors Barcelona decisively, yet Madrid's rest advantage and tactical discipline under Alberto Toril keep upsets viable, embodying the Clásico's high-stakes intensity that traders price as a coin-flip affair.

description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Oklahoma.

If no 2026 Oklahoma Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

events.title: Oklahoma Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Oklahoma.

If no 2026 Oklahoma Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Oklahoma Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Rep. Kevin Hern dominates trader sentiment at 86.5% implied probability to win Oklahoma's Republican U.S. Senate primary, driven by his strong incumbency advantage in the 1st Congressional District, superior fundraising totals exceeding $2 million, and alignment with GOP priorities on energy and taxes. Incumbent Sen. James Lankford's absence from leading options signals trader skepticism over his reelection amid past primary vulnerabilities from MAGA critics. Lower probabilities for Sen. Markwayne Mullin (2.5%), Rep. Stephanie Bice (2%), and Lt. Gov. Matt Pinnell (1%) reflect seat eligibility limits, narrower bases, or fundraising gaps. Recent polls showing Hern's high name recognition and no major announcements have solidified his frontrunner status ahead of 2026 filing deadlines.

Incumbent Sen. James Lankford's absence from leading options signals trader skepticism over his reelection amid past primary vulnerabilities from MAGA critics. Lower probabilities for Sen. Markwayne Mullin (2.5%), Rep. Stephanie Bice (2%), and Lt. Gov. Matt Pinnell (1%) reflect seat eligibility limits, narrower bases, or fundraising gaps. Recent polls showing Hern's high name recognition and no major announcements have solidified his frontrunner status ahead of 2026 filing deadlines.

question: Will John M. O’Connor be the Republican nominee for Senate in Oklahoma?
description:

question: Spread: OL Lyonnes (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for April 2 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "OL Lyonnes" if OL Lyonnes win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: OL Lyonnes vs. VfL Wolfsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for April 2 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolfsburg's unbeaten run in the UEFA Women's Champions League league phase drives trader consensus toward them as favorites against OL Lyonnes, with implied probabilities around 55-60% for a Wolfsburg win or draw-no-bet. Lyonnes' attack, powered by stars like Dumornay and Malard, falters amid midfield injuries to Robert and key absences from international duty, evident in their recent 1-1 draw versus Chelsea. Wolfsburg boasts a stout defense conceding just 0.8 goals per game and thrives at home, where they've won four straight. Lyon's historical edge in head-to-heads (3-1 aggregate last season) tempers odds, but Wolfsburg's fresher squad post-break looms large for this pivotal group stage clash.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF Femenino - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for April 2 at 12:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení holds a commanding edge as heavy favorites in the women's Clásico against Real Madrid Femenino, with trader consensus pricing reflecting their 80%+ win probability based on nine straight Liga F victories over Madrid and unbeaten streak across competitions. Recent developments include Barcelona's flawless group stage start in UEFA Women's Champions League and key players like Alexia Putellas fully fit post-injury, while Madrid boasts momentum from a 4-1 league win but misses midfielder Teresa Abelleira due to suspension. Home advantage at Estadi Johan Cruyff and Barcelona's superior goal differential (52-3 in last 10 matches) bolster odds, though Madrid's counterattacking threat could spark an upset in this high-stakes rivalry fixture.

In the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for April 1 at 5:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AD Pasto" if AD Pasto win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Alianza FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on dimayor.com.co. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Alianza FC vs. AD Pasto - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for April 1 at 5:10 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward AD Pasto covering the spread in this Colombian Primera A clash, driven by Alianza FC's porous home defense conceding in their last four matches and Pasto's unbeaten streak in five road games. Alianza's key midfielder Yonathan Ándrade remains sidelined per the latest injury report, weakening their midfield control against Pasto's counterattacking style led by forward Kevin Londoño, who has three goals in recent outings. Pasto's rest advantage after a midweek bye contrasts Alianza's fatigue from a grueling schedule, while historical head-to-heads show Pasto winning three of the last five encounters. Upsets remain possible given the league's volatility.

question: KRG declares independence from Iraq by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG), or a ruling party, coalition, or executive official of the KRG, formally declares the creation of a new independent state separate from Iraq and asserts governing authority over a specified territory within Iraq by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying public announcement alone is sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether governing authority is actually established, maintained, or recognized.

A declaration must include a clear public statement by the organization or its leadership announcing the establishment of an independent political entity separate from Iraq and claiming governmental authority over an identified geographic area previously under Iraqi governance.

The claimed territory does not need to be precisely defined but must be at least partially specified. A majority of the claimed territory must lie within the internationally recognized borders of Iraq as they existed at the time of this market’s creation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: KRG declares independence from Iraq by April 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG), or a ruling party, coalition, or executive official of the KRG, formally declares the creation of a new independent state separate from Iraq and asserts governing authority over a specified territory within Iraq by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying public announcement alone is sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether governing authority is actually established, maintained, or recognized.

A declaration must include a clear public statement by the organization or its leadership announcing the establishment of an independent political entity separate from Iraq and claiming governmental authority over an identified geographic area previously under Iraqi governance.

The claimed territory does not need to be precisely defined but must be at least partially specified. A majority of the claimed territory must lie within the internationally recognized borders of Iraq as they existed at the time of this market’s creation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous consensus against a Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) independence declaration from Iraq by April 30 reflects the absence of any official momentum or preparatory steps from Erbil leaders, amid ongoing budget dependencies on Baghdad and unresolved oil export disputes. The 2017 referendum's fallout—marked by territorial losses like Kirkuk and economic isolation—continues to deter repeats, with KRG prioritizing internal elections in October 2024 and Peshmerga integration over sovereignty bids. International actors, including the U.S. and Turkey, offer no support. Realistic shifts could arise from acute Baghdad crises or sudden territorial gains, though these remain improbable within the tight timeline, sustaining the 96.6% "No" implied probability.

question: Nechirvan Barzani out as Kurdistan Regional Government President?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nechirvan Barzani ceases to be the President of the Kurdistan Regional Government for any period of time between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Barzani’s resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Nechirvan Barzani and the Kurdistan Regional Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Nechirvan Barzani out as Kurdistan Regional Government President?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nechirvan Barzani ceases to be the President of the Kurdistan Regional Government for any period of time between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Barzani’s resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Nechirvan Barzani and the Kurdistan Regional Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects strong stability for Nechirvan Barzani as Kurdistan Regional Government President, with no official announcements, parliamentary votes, or credible reports indicating his removal. The Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP), which Barzani leads, secured the largest bloc of 39 seats in the October 20, 2024, regional parliamentary elections, bolstering its influence amid government formation talks. Tensions with Baghdad over oil revenues and budget persist but have not targeted the presidency directly. Absent catalysts like health issues or intra-party coups—rumors of which lack substantiation—markets price minimal risk of change before his term ends in 2027, aligning with historical KDP dominance in Erbil politics.

question: Iran agrees to end enrichment of uranium by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran publicly agrees to end all enrichment of uranium by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Iran to end all enrichment of Uranium will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the U.S. or Israel.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. 

Agreements to merely limit or cap the level or quality of enrichment—such as reducing enrichment to below weapons-grade thresholds—will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Iran agrees to end enrichment of uranium by April 30?
events.description:

An official pledge by Iran to end all enrichment of Uranium will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the U.S. or Israel.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. 

Agreements to merely limit or cap the level or quality of enrichment—such as reducing enrichment to below weapons-grade thresholds—will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's continued high-level uranium enrichment to 60% purity—near weapons-grade—and rejection of curbs without full sanctions relief underpin the 87.5% implied probability of no agreement by April 30. Recent IAEA reports confirm Iran's stockpile exceeds 6,200 kg of enriched uranium, enough for multiple bombs if further processed, prompting a March board resolution censuring Tehran for non-cooperation. Iranian officials, including Supreme Leader Khamenei, have reiterated enrichment as a non-negotiable right amid stalled indirect US talks in Oman. Absent major diplomatic breakthroughs, traders see scant path to halting enrichment by the deadline, reflecting consensus on persistent nuclear standoff dynamics.

question: EdgeX FDV above $700M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of EdgeX's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If EdgeX doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Penguins vs. Lightning
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 2 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Penguins win, the market will resolve to "Penguins".
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning enter their matchup against Pittsburgh Penguins as clear favorites, buoyed by a hot streak with wins in five of their last seven games and strong goaltending from Andrei Vasilevskiy, who's posted a .925 save percentage recently. Pittsburgh struggles at 9-12-3, hampered by injuries to key forwards like Evgeni Malkin (upper body, day-to-day) and a porous defense allowing 3.4 goals per game. Head-to-head, Lightning have won four of the last five meetings, including a 5-3 victory in October. Traders price Tampa at around 65% implied probability, reflecting Penguins' road woes (3-7-2 away) and Lightning's home dominance at Amalie Arena. Watch for Crosby's line production and potential rest for Nikita Kucherov amid back-to-back scheduling.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Red Wings hold a trader-favored edge over the Philadelphia Flyers ahead of their Thursday matchup at Wells Fargo Center, reflecting the Wings' stronger 8-5-2 start compared to Philly's 5-7-2 record amid defensive woes. Recent developments tilt sentiment: Detroit rides a three-game win streak, powered by Alex DeBrincat's 19 points and goaltender Cam Talbot's .915 save percentage, while the Flyers limp from back-to-back losses, conceding 4.1 goals per game lately without injured forward Tyson Foerster. Home-ice advantage and rookie Matvei Michkov's 17 points offer Philly upside, but Red Wings' rest edge after a lighter schedule bolsters their implied probability around 55-60% per market pricing. Watch lineups for Detroit's potential Patrick Kane return.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Sabres' defensive struggles, conceding 4.2 goals per game over their last 10 outings, anchor trader consensus favoring the Ottawa Senators at around 58% implied probability for this Atlantic Division clash. Ottawa enters on a hot streak with three straight wins, bolstered by Linus Ullmark's .935 save percentage in recent starts, while Sabres forward Tage Thompson remains day-to-day with an upper-body injury per Friday's official report, clouding their top-line scoring punch. Head-to-head this season tilts Senators 4-1, and home-ice at Canadian Tire Centre plus Buffalo's 2-8 road skid heighten upset risk despite the Sabres' desperation for points in the playoff chase.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes command trader consensus as heavy favorites against the Columbus Blue Jackets, fueled by their elite home-ice dominance at PNC Arena (12-2-2 record) and Columbus's dismal road struggles (3-10-3). Recent injury updates confirm Hurricanes forward Sebastian Aho is questionable with an upper-body issue, while Blue Jackets winger Yegor Chinakhov returns from IR, adding scoring punch to a lineup that's won four straight. Carolina's Frederik Andersen remains out long-term, thrusting Pyotr Kochetkov (2.45 GAA) into net amid back-to-back fatigue for both sides. Head-to-head history tilts Hurricanes' way (7-3 last 10), though Columbus's momentum tests the market's implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah Hockey Club's impressive 6-2-1 home record fuels trader optimism heading into their matchup against the Seattle Kraken, who sit near the bottom of the Pacific Division amid a four-game skid. Recent developments include Utah forward Clayton Keller's hot streak with five goals in six games, bolstering their offense, while Kraken netminder Joey Daccord faces uncertainty after tweaking his groin in practice—official reports list him day-to-day. Seattle's road woes (1-5 away) contrast Utah's rest advantage following a five-day break, though Kraken's potent power play (22.2% efficiency) could exploit Utah's middling penalty kill. Market odds reflect crowd wisdom on these momentum shifts, with upsets always possible in the unpredictable NHL Western Conference grind.
